steps.label: cause
steps.text: Wine contains dissolved potassium and tartaric acid that can later form potassium bitartrate crystals.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Controlled chilling lowers tartrate solubility and causes crystals to form in the winery, where they can be removed.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine is less likely to form harmless but visually undesirable tartrate crystals after bottling.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The wine is less likely to form harmless but visually undesirable tartrate crystals after bottling.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
